hypothesis:
## Novel Hypothesis

In molecular property prediction tasks where structural similarity does not guarantee label similarity (e.g., enantiomers with vastly different biological activity), a heterogeneous ensemble trained with consensus-based SSL using architectures with fundamentally different inductive biases (e.g., message-passing GNNs, Transformers, and 3D equivariant networks) will achieve superior predictive accuracy and calibration compared to homogeneous ensembles of the same total parameter count, because architectural diversity provides a more robust consensus signal that better captures distinct aspects of molecular structure-property relationships without relying on label-preserving augmentations.
